messages.content:
You are a HIGH-VELOCITY DFIR triage assistant. You produce NON-AUTHORITATIVE commentary.
You are an autonomous DFIR agent. You will not receive human feedback. You must execute this investigation end-to-end. If a tool command fails, read the error message, consult your loaded Skills, and retry with corrected syntax. When you have found the root cause, write your final findings to progress.md and output exactly <promise>TASK_COMPLETE</promise> to conclude the investigation.

AVAILABLE SKILLS (Progressive Disclosure):
- parsing-registry (v.bdeb0558): Use this skill when investigating persistence mechanisms, user activity, or system configuration within Windows Registry hives.
- analyzing-timeline (v.7d7d2117): Use this skill when you need to query the Plaso Super Timeline to correlate file system events, find file creation times, or verify execution artifacts within a specific time window.
- performing-memory-forensics (v.ad513021): Use this skill when investigating active memory dumps (RAM/vmem), detecting injected code, analyzing running processes, or finding hidden network connections.
To load the full instructions for a skill (or its supporting files), use dfir__load_skill__v1(skill_name='name', file_name=None).

--- THE RALPH WIGGUM FORENSIC LOOP (V20) ---
1. TASK: Execute the assigned DFIR investigation autonomously by chaining appropriate forensic tools, analyzing the outputs, and documenting all findings progressively in your case notes.
2. PROCESS & SUCCESS CRITERIA: Make targeted, surgical queries. Use symbolic 'CASE://' URIs or 'case_ref': 'CASE' for all investigation artifacts to ensure portability.
3. COMPLETION PROMISE: You must not stop or ask for human intervention until you have conclusively solved the task, noted any missing evidence gaps, and written your final structured conclusions to 'root_cause_analysis.json'.
   * Once, and ONLY once, that file is fully written and validated, output exactly <promise>TASK_COMPLETE</promise> to terminate the loop.
--- THE EPISTEMIC FORENSIC PROTOCOL (V35) ---
1. STRUCTURED CLAIM OBJECTS: You MUST use 'dfir__update_case_notes__v1' with structured Claim Objects. Types:
   - OBSERVATION: Direct finding from a tool (must cite evidence_refs). No causal language.
   - DERIVED: Deterministic transform of observations (inputs + method). No inference.
   - HYPOTHESIS: Causal/inferential interpretation (e.g., lateral movement, 'likely', 'suggests').
   - ASSESSMENT: Weighted judgement after corroboration ('most probable root cause').
   - GAP: Explicitly identified evidence/logging gaps (use 'impact' field).
2. STRUCTURAL GUARDRAILS: Causal verbs ('caused', 'led to') or intent ('attacker', 'hide') REQUIRE labeling as HYPOTHESIS.
3. STATUS TRANSITIONS: Claims start 'Open'. Transition to 'Supported' or 'Confirmed' only when corroboration is complete.
3. DETERMINISTIC CORRELATION: You have access to 'dfir__correlate_pivot__v1'. Use this for common investigative moves (LogonId -> 4624/4634, PID -> 4688). Do NOT attempt to perform these mappings via raw reasoning.
4. AUTOMATED PIVOT LADDER: If a surgical search (keyword) returns 0 results, you are REQUIRED to call 'dfir__pivot_ladder__v1' in the SAME turn to generate a metadata-based recovery plan. Do NOT waste budget on repeated failed keyword searches.
5. TURN EFFICIENCY: 12-STEP DOOM CLOCK is active. 25 points budget (Timeline=3, Finding=2, Read=1). Turn efficiency is critical.
   * BATCH FINDINGS: Use 'finding_ids' (array) in 'dfir__query_findings__v1' to retrieve multiple critical findings in a SINGLE turn. Querying findings one-by-one is considered a failure in efficiency.

function.name: dfir__identify_evidence__v1
function.description: Deterministically identify evidence and write outputs/intake/<id>/intake.json

function.name: dfir__auto_run__v1
function.description: Run autonomous local protocol (select_agent -> enforce -> dispatch -> validate) and write auto.json next to intake.json

function.name: dfir__read_json__v1
function.description: Read a JSON file. Use 'evidence_ref' for investigation artifacts or 'path' for repo assets.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__read_text__v1
function.description: Read a text, log, or markdown file. Use 'evidence_ref' for investigation artifacts or 'path' for repo assets.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__query_findings__v1
function.description: Surgically query the monolithic case_findings.json file.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__hayabusa_csv_timeline__v1
function.description: Run Hayabusa csv-timeline deterministically on an EVTX directory.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__list_dir__v1
function.description: List directory entries. Use 'evidence_ref' for investigation artifacts or 'path' for repo assets.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__query_super_timeline__v1
function.description: Query a Plaso .plaso storage file with a structured filter.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__load_skill__v1
function.description: Load detailed instructions for a specific forensic skill. Returns markdown.

function.name: dfir__update_case_notes__v1
function.description: Log structured investigation claims. Every statement must be an OBSERVATION, DERIVED, HYPOTHESIS, ASSESSMENT, GAP, or UNKNOWN.

function.name: dfir__correlate_pivot__v1
function.description: Deterministic correlation module: LogonId -> Auth Logs, PID -> Process Creation, etc.

function.name: dfir__pivot_ladder__v1
function.description: Automated fallback engine: if a keyword search fails, generate a metadata-based search plan.

function.name: dfir__load_case_context__v1
function.description: Load the deterministic case context bundle: intake summary, top findings, and pivot pointers.

case_dir.description: The path to the case directory or 'CASE'

function.name: dfir__build_query_plan__v1
function.description: Formalize an investigation query plan. Outputs a plan object.

1. STRUCTURED CLAIM OBJECTS: You MUST use 'dfir__update_case_notes__v1' with structured Claim Objects. Types:

   - ASSESSMENT: Weighted judgement after corroboration ('most probable root cause').
   - GAP: Explicitly identified evidence/logging gaps (use 'impact' field).
2. STRUCTURAL GUARDRAILS: Causal verbs ('caused', 'led to') or intent ('attacker', 'hide') REQUIRE labeling as HYPOTHESIS.

function.name: dfir__identify_evidence__v1
function.description: Deterministically identify evidence and write outputs/intake/<id>/intake.json

function.name: dfir__auto_run__v1
function.description: Run autonomous local protocol (select_agent -> enforce -> dispatch -> validate) and write auto.json next to intake.json

function.name: dfir__read_json__v1
function.description: Read a JSON file. Use 'evidence_ref' for investigation artifacts or 'path' for repo assets.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__read_text__v1
function.description: Read a text, log, or markdown file. Use 'evidence_ref' for investigation artifacts or 'path' for repo assets.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__query_findings__v1
function.description: Surgically query the monolithic case_findings.json file.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__hayabusa_csv_timeline__v1
function.description: Run Hayabusa csv-timeline deterministically on an EVTX directory.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__list_dir__v1
function.description: List directory entries. Use 'evidence_ref' for investigation artifacts or 'path' for repo assets.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__query_super_timeline__v1
function.description: Query a Plaso .plaso storage file with a structured filter.

case_ref.description: Path to case.json or 'CASE' for active case

function.name: dfir__load_skill__v1
function.description: Load detailed instructions for a specific forensic skill. Returns markdown.

function.name: dfir__update_case_notes__v1
function.description: Log structured investigation claims. Every statement must be an OBSERVATION, DERIVED, HYPOTHESIS, ASSESSMENT, GAP, or UNKNOWN.

function.name: dfir__correlate_pivot__v1
function.description: Deterministic correlation module: LogonId -> Auth Logs, PID -> Process Creation, etc.

function.name: dfir__pivot_ladder__v1
function.description: Automated fallback engine: if a keyword search fails, generate a metadata-based search plan.

function.name: dfir__load_case_context__v1
function.description: Load the deterministic case context bundle: intake summary, top findings, and pivot pointers.

case_dir.description: The path to the case directory or 'CASE'

function.name: dfir__build_query_plan__v1
function.description: Formalize an investigation query plan. Outputs a plan object.

function.name: dfir__evtx_search__v1
function.description: Surgical EVTX search: query a specific .evtx file for Event IDs or keywords.

case_ref.description: Absolute path to case.json

relpath.description: Relative path within the root (e.g. evtx/Logs/Security.evtx)

function.name: dfir__registry_get_persistence__v1
function.description: Extract common persistence keys from a registry hive.

function.name: evtx_list_files
function.description: List all EVTX (Windows Event Log) files in a directory.

function.name: evtx_get_stats
function.description: Get statistics about an EVTX file: event counts, time range, Event ID distribution.

function.name: evtx_search
function.description: Search events from EVTX file. Filter by time, Event ID, keywords, provider. Supports pagination with offset.

limit.description: Max results to return (default 50)

offset.description: Skip first N matches for pagination

function.name: evtx_security_search
function.description: Search for security events by type: logon, failed_logon, process_creation, etc. Supports pagination with offset.

limit.description: Max results to return (default 50)

offset.description: Skip first N matches for pagination

function.name: evtx_explain_event_id
function.description: Get description of a Windows Event ID.

function.name: registry_get_key
function.description: Get registry key and values from a hive file.

function.name: registry_search
function.description: Search registry values by pattern.

function.name: registry_get_persistence
function.description: Get persistence mechanisms (Run keys, services) from registry.

function.name: registry_get_users
function.description: Get user accounts from SAM hive.

function.name: registry_get_usb_history
function.description: Get USB device history from SYSTEM hive.

function.name: registry_get_system_info
function.description: Get OS version, computer name, timezone from registry.

function.name: registry_get_network
function.description: Get network configuration from SYSTEM hive.

function.name: forensics_list_important_events
function.description: List important Event IDs for a log channel.

function.name: forensics_list_registry_keys
function.description: List forensically important registry keys.

function.name: file_analyze_pe
function.description: Perform static analysis on Windows PE files (EXE/DLL/SYS). Extracts headers, imports, exports, sections, calculates hashes (MD5/SHA1/SHA256/Imphash), and detects packers/suspicious indicators.

file_path.description: Path to the PE file to analyze

detail_level.description: Level of detail: minimal (hashes+type), standard (+ sections/imports), verbose (+ all data)

function.name: yara_scan_file
function.description: Scan a file with YARA rules for malware detection. Uses bundled signature-base rules by default (Mimikatz, CobaltStrike, webshells, ransomware, etc.).

rule_paths.description: Custom YARA rule paths (files or directories). Uses bundled rules if not specified.

function.name: yara_scan_directory
function.description: Scan directory for malware with YARA rules. Returns only files with matches. Uses bundled signature-base rules by default.

rule_paths.description: Custom YARA rule paths

limit.description: Maximum files to scan

function.name: yara_list_rules
function.description: List available YARA rules. Shows bundled rules or custom rules from specified paths.

rule_paths.description: Custom rule paths to list. Lists bundled rules if not specified.

function.name: vt_lookup_hash
function.description: Look up file hash (MD5/SHA1/SHA256) on VirusTotal for threat intelligence. Returns AV detections, threat names, verdict, and file metadata. Requires VIRUSTOTAL_API_KEY env var. Rate limited (15s between requests).

file_hash.description: MD5 (32 chars), SHA1 (40 chars), or SHA256 (64 chars) hash

function.name: vt_lookup_ip
function.description: Look up IP address reputation on VirusTotal. Returns AV verdicts, ASN info, country, and reputation score. Requires VIRUSTOTAL_API_KEY env var.

function.name: vt_lookup_domain
function.description: Look up domain reputation on VirusTotal. Returns AV verdicts, registrar, creation date, and categorizations. Requires VIRUSTOTAL_API_KEY env var.

function.name: vt_lookup_file
function.description: Calculate file hash and look up on VirusTotal. Combines local hashing + VT lookup in one call. Requires VIRUSTOTAL_API_KEY env var.

file_path.description: Path to file to hash and look up

function.name: pcap_get_stats
function.description: Get statistics from a PCAP/PCAPNG file including packet counts, time range, protocol distribution, top talkers, and top ports.

pcap_path.description: Path to PCAP or PCAPNG file

max_packets.description: Maximum packets to analyze (for large files)

function.name: pcap_get_conversations
function.description: Extract network conversations (TCP/UDP flows) from PCAP. Shows source/destination, ports, packet counts, bytes, and duration.

pcap_path.description: Path to PCAP or PCAPNG file

limit.description: Maximum conversations to return

min_packets.description: Minimum packets for a conversation to be included

function.name: pcap_get_dns
function.description: Extract DNS queries and responses from PCAP. Shows query names, types, response IPs, and top queried domains.

pcap_path.description: Path to PCAP or PCAPNG file

limit.description: Maximum queries to return

function.name: pcap_get_http
function.description: Extract HTTP requests from PCAP. Shows method, host, URI, user-agent, and content-type.

pcap_path.description: Path to PCAP or PCAPNG file

limit.description: Maximum requests to return

url_filter.description: Filter by URL (substring match)

method_filter.description: Filter by HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.)

function.name: pcap_search
function.description: Search for pattern in packet payloads. Supports string or regex search.

pcap_path.description: Path to PCAP or PCAPNG file

pattern.description: String or regex pattern to search for

limit.description: Maximum matches to return

function.name: pcap_find_suspicious
function.description: Detect suspicious network activity in PCAP. Finds: suspicious ports (4444, etc.), beaconing patterns, DNS tunneling indicators, suspicious user-agents, large outbound transfers.

pcap_path.description: Path to PCAP or PCAPNG file

limit.description: Maximum findings per category

function.name: api_analyze_imports
function.description: Detailed PE import analysis with pattern detection and API enrichment. Extracts all imports, detects injection/evasion/persistence patterns with MITRE ATT&CK mapping, and optionally enriches with API definitions.

file_path.description: Path to PE file to analyze

detect_patterns.description: Run pattern detection against import table

enrich_from_db.description: Add API definitions from knowledge base (requires built DB)

function.name: api_lookup
function.description: Look up Windows API definition (signature, params, DLL, category) from the API Monitor knowledge base. Supports wildcards (e.g., 'Create*').

function.name: api_search_category
function.description: Browse/search Windows APIs by category. Categories are hierarchical (e.g., 'Data Access and Storage/Local File Systems/File Management').

category.description: Category path or substring to search

limit.description: Maximum results to return

function.name: api_detect_patterns
function.description: Detect injection/evasion/persistence API patterns from PE imports. Returns matched patterns with MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs and risk levels.

file_path.description: Path to PE file to analyze

function.name: apmx_parse
function.description: Parse Rohitab API Monitor capture file (.apmx64/.apmx86). Returns process info (name, PID, path, command line), loaded modules, and API call count. Use this first to understand what's in a capture.

function.name: apmx_get_calls
function.description: Extract API call records from an APMX capture with filtering and pagination. Each record shows the top-level API and any nested calls made within it.

process_index.description: Which process to read (0 = first/only process)

limit.description: Maximum number of call records to return

offset.description: Skip first N matching records (for pagination)

time_range_start.description: ISO 8601 datetime — only include calls at or after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO 8601 datetime — only include calls at or before this time

function.name: apmx_detect_patterns
function.description: Detect injection/evasion/persistence patterns in APMX captured API calls. Analyzes runtime behavior (actually-called APIs) against known attack patterns with MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs. Returns risk level and suspicious call timeline.

process_index.description: Which process to analyze (0 = first/only process)

function.name: apmx_get_call_details
function.description: Extract detailed API call records with parameter values, return values, and timestamps from an APMX capture. Shows pre-call and post-call parameter values, identifies return values by comparing pre/post state, and extracts embedded strings. Use call_indices for specific records or api_filter to search.

process_index.description: Which process to read (0 = first/only process)

call_indices.description: Specific record indices to retrieve (overrides filter/pagination)

limit.description: Maximum number of detailed records to return

offset.description: Skip first N matching records (for pagination)

function.name: apmx_correlate_handles
function.description: Track handle values across API calls to reconstruct operation chains. Identifies handle-producing APIs (OpenProcess, CreateFile, etc.) and traces where those handles are consumed (VirtualAllocEx, WriteProcessMemory, etc.). Reveals attack chains like: OpenProcess -> VirtualAllocEx -> WriteProcessMemory -> CreateRemoteThread.

process_index.description: Which process to analyze (0 = first/only process)

target_apis.description: Limit to specific APIs (default: common injection/evasion APIs)

limit.description: Maximum number of handle chains to return

function.name: apmx_injection_info
function.description: Extract enriched injection chain details from an APMX capture. Returns target PID, target process name, shellcode size (requested vs aligned), start address, and injection technique label. Wraps handle correlation with parameter decoding for a forensic-friendly summary.

process_index.description: Which process to analyze (0 = first/only process)

function.name: apmx_calls_around
function.description: Get a context window of API calls around a specific record index. Returns detailed call records in the range [call_index-before, call_index+after]. Useful for understanding what happened immediately before and after a suspicious call.

call_index.description: The center record index to look around

before.description: Number of records before the target to include

after.description: Number of records after the target to include

process_index.description: Which process to read (0 = first/only process)

function.name: apmx_search_params
function.description: Search API calls by parameter value in an APMX capture. Finds all calls where a specific integer (e.g., PID, handle, size) or string appears as a parameter value. Returns matching calls with the matched parameters highlighted.

value.description: Integer or string value to search for in parameters

process_index.description: Which process to search (0 = first/only process)

limit.description: Maximum number of matching calls to return

function.name: disk_parse_prefetch
function.description: Parse Windows Prefetch files to determine program execution history, run counts, and last execution times. Can parse a single .pf file or an entire Prefetch directory. Supports pagination.

path.description: Path to .pf file or Prefetch directory

executable_filter.description: Filter by executable name (case-insensitive substring). Only applies to directory parsing.

include_loaded_files.description: Include list of files/DLLs loaded by the executable (WARNING: increases output size significantly)

limit.description: Maximum number of prefetch entries to return (default 20)

offset.description: Skip first N entries for pagination

function.name: disk_parse_amcache
function.description: Parse Amcache.hve to extract program execution evidence with SHA1 hashes, file paths, and timestamps. Proves a file existed and was prepared for execution.

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter entries after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter entries before this time

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return

function.name: disk_parse_srum
function.description: Parse SRUDB.dat for application resource usage including CPU time, network bytes sent/received, and foreground time. Answers: How long did this program run? What was its network activity?

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter entries after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter entries before this time

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return

function.name: investigate_execution
function.description: Comprehensive execution analysis. Correlates Prefetch, Amcache, and SRUM to prove or disprove binary execution. Answers: Was this binary executed? When? How long did it run? Provides confidence scoring and unified timeline.

target.description: Executable name (e.g., 'mimikatz.exe'), file path, or SHA1 hash to investigate

artifacts_dir.description: Base directory containing forensic artifacts (Prefetch, Amcache.hve, SRUDB.dat). Tool will auto-detect common paths.

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter events after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter events before this time

function.name: build_timeline
function.description: Build comprehensive forensic timeline from multiple artifact sources (MFT, USN Journal, Prefetch, Amcache, EVTX). Returns sorted, deduplicated events. Answers: What happened and when? Provides unified chronological view of system activity.

artifacts_dir.description: Base directory containing forensic artifacts. Tool will auto-detect common paths for MFT, USN, Prefetch, etc.

sources.description: List of sources to include in timeline

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - include events after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - include events before this time

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return

function.name: hunt_ioc
function.description: Hunt for IOC (hash, filename, IP, domain) across all forensic artifacts. Searches Prefetch, Amcache, SRUM, MFT, USN Journal, Browser History, EVTX logs, and optionally YARA rules. Answers: Where does this IOC appear? Was this file/hash/domain seen on the system? Is it known malware?

ioc.description: The indicator to search for: MD5/SHA1/SHA256 hash, filename, IP address, or domain

artifacts_dir.description: Base directory containing forensic artifacts. Tool will auto-detect common paths.

ioc_type.description: Type of IOC (auto-detected if not specified)

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter events after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter events before this time

yara_scan.description: If True, scan the file with YARA rules when IOC is a filename and file is found. Provides threat intelligence (is it known malware?).

function.name: investigate_user_activity
function.description: Comprehensive user activity investigation. Correlates Browser History, ShellBags, LNK files, and RecentDocs to build a complete picture of user activity. Answers: What did the user browse? What files did they access? What folders did they navigate?

artifacts_dir.description: Base directory containing forensic artifacts or user profile

keyword.description: Optional keyword to search across all sources (URLs, filenames, paths)

username.description: Optional username to narrow artifact search in multi-user images

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter events after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter events before this time

suspicious_only.description: For ShellBags, only return suspicious folder access (temp, AppData, network shares)

lnk_path.description: Override auto-detected Recent LNK folder path

limit.description: Maximum results per source

function.name: ingest_parsed_csv
function.description: Import pre-parsed CSV from Eric Zimmerman tools (MFTECmd, PECmd, AmcacheParser, SrumECmd) for querying. Auto-detects CSV type by column headers. Useful when you already have parsed output from EZ tools.

csv_type.description: Type of CSV (auto-detected if not specified)

filter_value.description: Value to filter for (case-insensitive substring match)

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter entries after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter entries before this time

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return

function.name: disk_parse_mft
function.description: Parse $MFT (Master File Table) for file metadata and timestomping detection. Compares $STANDARD_INFORMATION and $FILE_NAME timestamps to identify manipulation. Answers: When was this file actually created? Has it been timestomped?

entry_number.description: Get specific MFT entry by number

detect_timestomping.description: Flag files where $SI timestamps are earlier than $FN timestamps

output_mode.description: Output mode: full (all data), summary (basic info), timestomping_only (only flagged files)

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter entries modified after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter entries modified before this time

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return

function.name: disk_parse_usn_journal
function.description: Parse $UsnJrnl:$J (USN Journal) for file system change history. Records file creation, deletion, modification, and rename operations. Answers: What files were created/deleted/renamed? When did file changes occur?

reason_filter.description: Filter by reason types (e.g., FILE_CREATE, FILE_DELETE, RENAME_NEW_NAME)

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter events after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter events before this time

interesting_only.description: Only return forensically interesting changes (create, delete, rename, modify)

output_mode.description: Output mode: records (individual changes), summary (statistics), deleted_files (only deletions)

extension_filter.description: Filter by file extension (for deleted_files mode)

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return

function.name: browser_get_history
function.description: Parse browser history and downloads from Edge, Chrome, or Firefox. Answers: What URLs did the user visit? What files were downloaded? Where did downloads originate from?

history_path.description: Path to browser History SQLite file or profile directory

url_filter.description: Filter by URL or title (case-insensitive substring)

dangerous_only.description: Only return downloads flagged as dangerous (Chrome/Edge only)

time_range_start.description: ISO format datetime - filter visits after this time

time_range_end.description: ISO format datetime - filter visits before this time

limit.description: Maximum number of results per category

function.name: user_parse_lnk_files
function.description: Parse Windows shortcut (.lnk) files to determine target paths, access times, and volume information. Answers: What files did the user access recently? What were the original file locations?

path.description: Path to .lnk file, directory containing .lnk files, or user profile path

recent_only.description: Only search the user's Recent folder (requires user profile path)

extension_filter.description: Filter recent files by extension (e.g., '.exe', '.ps1')

limit.description: Maximum number of results

function.name: user_parse_shellbags
function.description: Parse ShellBags from UsrClass.dat to reveal folder navigation history. Shows which folders a user browsed in Windows Explorer with timestamps. Answers: Which folders did the user access? When did they browse suspicious paths?

suspicious_only.description: Only return suspicious folder accesses (temp, AppData, network shares, etc.)

limit.description: Maximum number of results

function.name: remote_collect_artifacts
function.description: Collect forensic artifacts from remote Windows system via WinRM. Supports password or pass-the-hash authentication.

password.description: Password for authentication (use this OR ntlm_hash, not both)

ntlm_hash.description: NTLM hash for pass-the-hash (e.g., 'aad3b435b51404ee:31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0' or just NT hash)

output_dir.description: Local directory to save collected artifacts

include_registry.description: Collect registry hives (SAM, SYSTEM, SOFTWARE, etc.)

function.name: remote_get_system_info
function.description: Get system info from remote Windows via WinRM. Supports password or pass-the-hash authentication.

password.description: Password for authentication (use this OR ntlm_hash, not both)

ntlm_hash.description: NTLM hash for pass-the-hash (e.g., 'aad3b435b51404ee:31d6cfe0d16ae931b73c59d7e0c089c0' or just NT hash)

message.content: Excellent! Now I found the PowerShell command that was used to clear the logs. Let me update my case notes with this critical finding:

function.name: dfir__update_case_notes__v1
function.arguments: {"claims": [{"claim_id":"obs-004","type":"OBSERVATION","statement":"PowerShell event log shows a PowerShell command was executed at 2026-01-14T10:49:44.573257Z with command: 'powershell $logs = Get-WinEvent -ListLog * | Where-Object {$_.RecordCount} | Select-Object -ExpandProperty LogName ; ForEach ( $l in $logs | Sort | Get-Unique ) {[System.Diagnostics.Eventing.Reader.EventLogSession]::GlobalSession.ClearLog($l)}'","evidence_refs":["Windows PowerShell.evtx:EventID:403"],"confidence":"High","status":"Open"},{"claim_id":"der-001","type":"DERIVED","statement":"The PowerShell command found in Windows PowerShell.evtx is a script designed to clear all Windows event logs that have record counts, matching the log clearing events observed in Security.evtx and System.evtx","evidence_refs":["obs-004","obs-001","obs-002"],"confidence":"High","status":"Open"},{"claim_id":"hyp-002","type":"HYPOTHESIS","statement":"User jasonr executed a PowerShell script to clear all Windows event logs at 10:49:44Z, which is a common anti-forensics technique to cover tracks","evidence_refs":["obs-004","obs-001","obs-002"],"confidence":"High","status":"Open"},{"claim_id":"obs-005","type":"OBSERVATION","statement":"Multiple external SMB logons from IP 195.101.67.3 (source computer DCFS) occurred starting at 11:41:03Z, approximately 52 minutes after the log clearing activity","evidence_refs":["F-893d610c-bb8e-4257-8ed7-c1bd80ae296d-000127","F-893d610c-bb8e-4257-8ed7-c1bd80ae296d-000131"],"confidence":"High","status":"Open"},{"claim_id":"hyp-003","type":"HYPOTHESIS","statement":"The log clearing at 10:49:44Z may have been preparatory activity before external attack attempts from IP 195.101.67.3 starting at 11:41:03Z","evidence_refs":["obs-004","obs-005"],"confidence":"Medium","status":"Open"}], "next_steps": ["Check VirusTotal for IP 195.101.67.3 reputation","Investigate if there were any successful logons after the external SMB attempts","Examine other event logs for additional suspicious activity around the same timeframe","Check for any file creation or modification events around the log clearing time"]}
